content: You didn't tell which Thunderbird version you are using. Anyhow, the Config Editor button is available in all Thunderbird versions. Here is a screen shot (see image) with the Config Editor button in my Thunderbird 151 version. If you are too lazy to go down all the way you can download the "Config Button" add-on https://services.addons.thunderbird.net/En-us/thunderbird/addon/config-button/ and install it via your Add-ons Manager. The "Config" button will then be displayed in Thunderbird toolbar.

content: Use the Global Search feature if you haven't already. If the results come up as a timeline, click the "show results as list" link. Right-click on the message that was found and choose <Open | Open Message in Containing folder> and you'll be taken to that folder in the Folder pane.

creator: westief

content:
I think the folders are start off in alphabetical order when you first set up the account, but the order is actually arbitrary and renaming them doesn't cause them to move themselves into alpha order. In recent versions of Thunderbird they can be manually reordered by dragging and dropping, and in version 150 and newer, there's the option to "Reset Folder Order", which will re-alphabetize them. That option is found in the right-click context menu for each account in the Folders List.

"Global Search" is the one that searches everything, rather than the Quick Search which only searches the folder you're in. It's the large search box that's up top by default, though that can be changed. Here's an article about how to use it. The system is isn't as flexible as I would like, but it does the basic job well.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/global-search
creator: rednoise

content:
You may have the Bitdefender Toolbar add-on installed. To remove it:

- open the Extensions Manager and if it's there, uninstall it > restart TB - check to see if it's still uninstalled - if it is gone and the message is still there, close TB and delete a registry key: - open the registry editor from an elevated command prompt: 

- close TB - open 'Run' (WinKey+R) - type cmd in the 'Open' box - press the Ctrl + Shift + Enter keys - type regedit > Enter

Then

Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies - double-click 'Policies' on the left to open the key - R. click 'Thunderbird' > Delete - close the registry editor - restart TB

If you don't have the BD Toolbar installed but have set BD to scan email, disable scanning and delete the registry key mentioned above.

content:
I don't know what you mean by "switch back to using Daily", but as I read it, issue appears to have to do specifically with Google.

A quick google search brings of some references to similar error messages that seem to have been been caused by anti-virus programs that have mail scanning features. Avast and AVG are frequent offenders. Here's a conversation about this from a few years ago. Does this look relevant with your situation?

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1389463

The fix in general seems to be to disable their email protection feature.

content:
Portable versions of Thunderbird can be found here:

https://portableapps.com/apps/internet/thunderbird_portable

https://portableapps.com/apps/internet/thunderbird-portable-esr

https://portableapps.com/apps/internet/thunderbird_portable/legacy

This must be a Portable issue, not a Thunderbird issue. Any 16-bit Thunderbird would be decades old, if there ever was one. Maybe it's an old Portable installer program that's causing the complaint. Most if all old versions of Thunderbird will run on Windows 11, though you probably don't want to run very old ones.

content:
Menu item <View | Sort By | Unthreaded>

or

Click the little "tree" icon in the message list column header to toggle between threaded and unthreaded. You may have to add that "thread" column first.

or

Click the “Message list display options” icon at the right side of the Message List Header, and choose <Sort By | Unthreaded>

For the default for new folders, see <Settings | General | Default Threading>.
creator: rednoise

content: If you set all computers up to use IMAP, everything you do on one machine will synchronize with the others. That is, new messages will download to them all, and messages you delete on one will delete on all. But you can set the travel laptops to only download full copies of the most recent messages, which would save time and disk space. Those settings are found in <Accounts Settings | Synchronization & Storage | Disk Space>. Local Folders, of course, live separately on each machine.
